metadata.raw_background: I’m a international student from Macao, now a year 2 preparing to studying Smart manufactoring, I have been in HKUST(GZ) for 2 years

responses.question: Could you briefly introduce yourself — your nationality, program, year of study, and how long you have been at HKUST(GZ)?
responses.answer: I’m a international student from Macao, now a year 2 preparing to studying Smart manufactoring, I have been in HKUST(GZ) for 2 years

responses.question: What was your first impression of student life at HKUST(GZ), especially socially?
responses.answer: Life is very good here, with a one-person bedroom, and dorm. I can make more friends which have the same hobby with me.

responses.question: How easy or difficult was it for you to make friends when you first arrived? Why?
responses.answer: Very easy, because the campus is small, and people will go around, so its very easy to make friend. (also with imt and clubs)

responses.question: Do you feel that you belong socially at HKUST(GZ)? Why or why not?
responses.answer: Yeah, I think I do

responses.question: Who do you mostly spend time with on campus — students from your own background, Chinese students, or a mix? What shaped that pattern?
responses.answer: Both, if they match my hobbies, and happy and willing to communicate with.

responses.question: Have you ever felt left out, disconnected, or excluded in social situations on campus? Can you describe a specific example?
responses.answer: Sometimes, espically in academic life.

responses.question: How would you describe your interactions with Chinese students at HKUST(GZ)?
responses.answer: A selection, I can go well with some of them

responses.question: What makes it easier to connect with Chinese students, and what makes it harder?
responses.answer: Maybe hobbies and sports, but the hard part is talking about academic life and some beliefs

responses.question: To what extent do language differences affect your social relationships here?
responses.answer: Nope, my chinese is good, so no language difference

responses.question: To what extent do cultural differences affect your comfort, confidence, or communication with others?
responses.answer: It doesn’t affect as well, because different regions will have their own cultures, so not a big deal for me

responses.question: Do you think students at HKUST(GZ) mix naturally across backgrounds, or do they mostly stay in separate groups? Why?
responses.answer: In my opinion I do think they tend to stay in their own separate groups

responses.question: Do you think your social experience at university has affected your academic life? How?
responses.answer: No, I think they are two different circustance

responses.question: Has your ability or inability to build friendships influenced your motivation, confidence, or study habits?
responses.answer: No, I tend to study myself, so it did not help my study alot

responses.question: How has your social experience affected your access to study groups, academic help, or useful information?
responses.answer: I tend to from groups with people whom I know well

responses.question: In group projects or collaborative assignments, have social or cultural dynamics affected your experience? Can you give an example?
responses.answer: Maybe sometimes, the way of communication is different, I prefer online meetings but most of them like to do it offline.

responses.question: Have you ever felt academically disadvantaged because you were not well connected socially? Why or why not?
responses.answer: No, but maybe sometimes you will miss some information because you didn’t join the communication

responses.question: Do you think students with stronger social networks generally perform better academically here? What makes you say that?
responses.answer: There is not a relationship between these two.

responses.question: Have you noticed any difference in your performance between UCUG and UFUG courses? What do you think explains that difference?
responses.answer: No I did not find big difference between these two

responses.question: Do factors like language, class participation, or social connections affect your grades in UCUG and UFUG courses differently? How?
responses.answer: UFUG may care more about participation and groupwork, while UCUG work more on grades and academic.

responses.question: What do you think are the main barriers preventing stronger integration between international and Chinese students at HKUST(GZ)?
responses.answer: Hobbies and the way of life and thinking

responses.question: Do you think the university does enough to help international students integrate socially and academically? Why or why not?
responses.answer: Yes, extra workshops and some clubs, but not much in academic life.

responses.question: What changes would you recommend to improve social integration and academic support for international students?
responses.answer: More communication and experience shared

responses.question: Is there anything about the international student experience at HKUST(GZ) that people usually overlook or misunderstand?
responses.answer: Weak academic foundation, need to work hard to keep on, and different way of thinking.

summary_bullets: International student from Macao in Year 2, preparing for Smart Manufacturing, with two years at HKUST(GZ).
Found it very easy to make friends because the campus is small and clubs create contact.
Feels a sense of social belonging and spends time with both Chinese and international students who share hobbies.
Says hobbies and sports help connection, while academic life and beliefs make deeper conversation harder.
Does not see language or culture as major personal barriers, but thinks students still remain in separate groups.
Separates social life and academic life, though weak communication can mean missing information.
Does not see a strong relationship between social networks and academic performance.
Thinks UFUG emphasizes participation and group work more, while UCUG focuses more on grades and academics.
